summary: The paper delivers a genuinely valuable metric audit: the universal control failure (shuffled > measured across all 5 domains on Gemma 2 2B JumpReLU SAEs) and the L0 phase transition (42.9% to 0.8%, CV < 10% across layers) are robust, novel findings that the mechanistic interpretability community needs. However, six critical issues prevent acceptance at a top venue. (1) DATA INTEGRITY: The paper reports CMI statistics from phase_transition_prediction.json (absorbed mean=0.687, U=41, p=0.042) rather than from the CMI estimation's own partition in cmi_estimation.json (absorbed mean=0.649, U=28, p=0.045). This is not a typo but a partition mismatch -- two different experiments define 'absorbed' differently, and the paper silently uses whichever numbers look better. (2) HEDGING CLASSIFICATION BIAS: The 98.6% hedging figure is near-guaranteed because the definition counts resolution at ANY higher L0 as hedging, but L0=176 resolves 99.2% of all false negatives trivially by allowing 8x more features. The paper never checks whether the specific parent latent fires at higher L0. (3) CMI DIMENSION INSTABILITY: rho=-0.383 at d'=10 reverses to rho=+0.048 at d'=20 and rho=+0.299 at d'=30. Bonferroni-corrected p=0.236. The d'=10 was not pre-registered. (4) PROBE QUALITY CONFOUND: Absorption correlates with probe F1 at rho=-0.67 (p<0.001), never controlled in CMI analysis. (5) THREE BLOCKING EXPERIMENTS UNEXECUTED: activation patching on 9 core words (the only possible causal evidence), control failure diagnosis (why does shuffled exceed measured?), and CMI replication at L0=22 with perfect probes. (6) THEORETICAL OVERCLAIMING: 'CMI Predicts Absorption Susceptibility' in the Section 6 title from p=0.236 corrected evidence. The paper's two solid pillars (metric audit + L0 phase transition) are undermined by inflated theoretical claims and unresolved data pipeline problems. Four of seven hypotheses are falsified. Score estimate: 6.5/10 (below acceptance threshold). Fixable in one iteration if the three blocking experiments are executed, data integrity is reconciled, hedging classification is tightened, and theoretical language is downgraded.

findings.description: DATA INTEGRITY -- PARTITION MISMATCH, NOT JUST TRANSCRIPTION ERROR: The paper reports absorbed mean CMI=0.687, Mann-Whitney U=41, p=0.042 (Abstract, Introduction, Section 6.2). These numbers come from phase_transition_prediction.json, which uses a different absorbed/non-absorbed partition than cmi_estimation.json (the experiment that actually estimated CMI). In cmi_estimation.json: absorbed mean=0.6492, U=28.0, p=0.04514. The partition difference: phase_transition_prediction defines absorption based on a different threshold or rate source, producing fewer absorbed letters and different group membership. This is worse than a transcription error -- the paper cherry-picks the experiment file whose partition produces more favorable test statistics. Additionally, three vocabulary sizes are used for the same task (1,092 in CMI, 1,196 in confound decomposition, 1,204 in first_letter_improved), producing two L0=82 absorption rates in the same paper (14.39% vs 15.96%). Per-letter word counts differ across experiments (letter D: 53/60/61). A reviewer auditing ANY of these numbers against source data will reject the paper.
findings.suggestion: Establish ONE canonical pipeline and partition for ALL first-letter analyses. The confound_decomposition_multi_l0 pipeline (1,196 words, 25 letters after excluding X) is the natural choice because it spans all four L0 values with consistent probes. Recompute CMI statistics using this canonical partition. Report the first_letter_improved rates ONLY as a robustness check in a footnote. Add a reconciliation table. Implement an automated validation script cross-checking paper numbers against source JSONs.

findings.description: HEDGING CLASSIFICATION IS BIASED BY DESIGN: The operational definition classifies a false negative as 'hedging' if the token ceases to be a false negative at ANY higher L0 value (41, 82, or 176). At L0=176, only 10 of 1,195 tokens remain as false negatives, meaning 99.2% of all tokens trivially 'resolve.' The classification does NOT check whether the specific parent latent (identified by the probe with cosine >= 0.025) fires at the higher L0. Multiple alternative resolution mechanisms exist: a different latent with incidental cosine alignment fires, the probe behavior changes under different activation patterns, or a compensatory feature reconstructs the parent direction. The 98.6% hedging figure is therefore an upper bound that would hold even if 0% of the resolution involved actual hedging. The maximum possible hierarchy-driven fraction at L0=22 is capped at 10/657 = 1.5% regardless of mechanism -- the classification is near-tautological.
findings.suggestion: Implement a strict hedging classification: a false negative at L0=22 is hedging ONLY if the specific parent-associated latent has activation > 0 at L0=176 for that token. Report BOTH permissive (98.6%) and strict classifications. If strict still shows >80% hedging, the claim is robust. If it drops substantially, the narrative must change. This is computationally trivial.

findings.description: CMI DIMENSION INSTABILITY IS A QUALITATIVE FAILURE: The CMI-absorption correlation at d'=10 (rho=-0.383, p=0.059) reverses sign at d'=20 (rho=+0.048), d'=30 (rho=+0.299), and d'=50 (rho=+0.197). This is not noise sensitivity -- it is a sign reversal, meaning the claimed theoretical relationship becomes the opposite at d'>=20. Bonferroni-corrected p=0.236. The methodology document listed d'={10,20,30,50} as 'sensitivity checks' without designating d'=10 as primary. The paper retroactively frames d'=10 as 'the primary analysis,' constituting post-hoc researcher degrees of freedom. Three competing explanations are equally plausible: (a) d'=10 captures the absorption-relevant subspace, (b) d'=10 is a statistical coincidence, (c) the k-NN MI estimator breaks down at d'>=20.
findings.suggestion: Provide an independent justification for d'=10 (eigenspectrum analysis). Or replicate at L0=22 with pre-registered d'=10. Or use dimension-agnostic MI estimator (MINE/KNIFE). At minimum, always report Bonferroni p=0.236. Retitle Section 6 from 'CMI Predicts...' to 'Exploratory CMI-Absorption Association.'

findings.description: PROBE QUALITY CONFOUND UNCONTROLLED IN CMI ANALYSIS: Per-letter absorption rate correlates with probe F1 at rho=-0.67 (p<0.001). The CMI-absorption correlation uses rates from L0=82 where only 10/25 letters pass F1>0.85. A confounding pathway: letters harder to probe have both inflated absorption (probe errors) and noisier CMI estimates. The paper never computes partial correlation controlling for probe F1, nor restricts analysis to quality-gated letters.
findings.suggestion: Compute: (1) partial rho(CMI, absorption | probe_F1), (2) correlation restricted to 10 letters with F1>0.85, (3) use L0=22 rates where all probes have F1=1.0. Option 3 eliminates the confound entirely and doubles as CMI replication.

findings.description: THREE BLOCKING EXPERIMENTS UNEXECUTED: (1) Activation patching on 9 persistent core words -- the ONLY metric-independent causal evidence for competitive exclusion, estimated 0.5-1 GPU-hour. (2) Control failure diagnosis -- analytical computation of expected shuffled rate plus reporting of already-computed threshold sensitivity results (ablation_threshold_sensitivity.json, 141KB). Zero additional GPU. (3) CMI replication at L0=22 with perfect probes (F1=1.0 for all 25 letters). Estimated 1 GPU-hour. Total: 1.5-2 GPU-hours plus analysis. The paper was written before these experiments, meaning claims exceed evidence.
findings.suggestion: Execute in priority order: (P1) Activation patching -- highest information gain, resolves the two-interpretation ambiguity. (P2) Report threshold sensitivity results -- zero compute, just analysis. (P3) CMI replication at L0=22 -- resolves probe quality confound and provides pre-registered test.

findings.description: SYSTEMATIC THEORETICAL OVERCLAIMING: Section 6 title: 'CMI Predicts Absorption Susceptibility' from p=0.236 corrected evidence with sign reversal. Abstract: 'predicts.' Introduction: 'first information-theoretic criterion.' Phase transition prediction is tautological (lambda from data). Binary classification accuracy 36% vs 64% chance baseline. Rank-order prediction non-significant (rho=+0.333, p=0.103). Successive refinement theorem conditions do not formally map onto SAE feature interactions. Overclaiming has recurred across iterations.
findings.suggestion: Downgrade systematically: 'predicts' -> 'shows suggestive association with'; 'criterion' -> 'preliminary diagnostic signal'; retitle Section 6; always report Bonferroni p alongside uncorrected; remove '10.2% error' claim or label as circular; note successive refinement connection is an analogy, not derivation.

findings.description: THRESHOLD SENSITIVITY RESULTS COMPUTED BUT UNREPORTED: The 5x4 grid was executed (ablation_threshold_sensitivity.json, 141KB) but the paper never reports it. This directly tests whether control failure is threshold-dependent (fixable by recalibration) or structural (fundamental). The methodology promises this analysis. Omitting completed, planned experiments creates selective reporting concern.
findings.suggestion: Include as table or figure. Key question: does tighter cosine (0.05) make measured > shuffled? Either answer strengthens the metric audit.

findings.description: TWO-INTERPRETATION AMBIGUITY UNRESOLVED: 'Metric does not transfer' conflates (a) JumpReLU genuinely has minimal absorption and (b) metric miscalibrated but absorption exists. Paper favors (b) without evidence. Activation patching is the discriminating test.
findings.suggestion: Add explicit two-interpretation paragraph in Section 7.2. Frame activation patching as decisive test. Replace 'miscalibrated' with neutral: 'output inconsistent with hierarchy-driven absorption.'

findings.description: CROSS-DOMAIN NOVELTY SELF-CONTRADICTORY: Claims 'first cross-domain measurements' but admits measurements are 'uninterpretable.' Also, 'Sparse but Wrong' (Chanin and Garriga-Alonso, 2025) already establishes hedging mechanism qualitatively.
findings.suggestion: Reframe novelty as 'first systematic metric audit on JumpReLU with controls.' Position relative to 'Sparse but Wrong': prior work establishes mechanism, this study quantifies its dominance across five domains.

findings.description: LETTERS S AND K ARE PROMINENT OUTLIERS: S has CMI=0.961 but absorption=31.4%; K has CMI=0.592 but absorption=0%. Both directly contradict the claimed pattern. No leave-one-out analysis reported. With n=25 and marginal significance, single outliers can drive results.
findings.suggestion: Compute and report leave-one-out sensitivity for all 25 letters. Discuss S and K as notable exceptions. Understanding why they defy the pattern is at least as informative as the pattern itself.

findings.description: NO DIAGNOSIS OF WHY CONTROLS FAIL: The headline finding lacks mechanistic explanation. Three untested candidates: permissive cosine threshold, JumpReLU dead-feature concentration, shuffled probes finding more candidates. Analytical computation (cosine distribution of random vectors against 16k decoder columns) is zero-GPU cost.
findings.suggestion: Sample 1,000 random unit vectors, count decoder columns with cosine >= 0.025. Compare with true probe candidate counts. If random directions find many candidates, the explanation is threshold permissiveness.

findings.description: CMI ESTIMATION LACKS STANDARD DIAGNOSTICS: No per-letter bootstrap CIs, convergence curves, k-sensitivity, or alternative estimator comparison. The sign reversal at d'>=20 may reflect estimator breakdown. k-NN MI with k=5, n=3,000, d'=10 is known to have bias.
findings.suggestion: Report bootstrap CIs per letter, convergence curves for 3 representative letters, sensitivity to k={3,5,10,20}. If bootstrap CIs overlap substantially between groups, the Mann-Whitney test is unreliable.

findings.description: STRUCTURAL REDUNDANCY: Control failure stated 4 times near-identically. Section 5.3 presents ~200 words on a fully confounded cross-model comparison. ~300 words could be freed for missing threshold sensitivity table.
findings.suggestion: Keep control failure in 2 places only. Compress Section 5.3 to 2-3 sentences. Use freed space for threshold sensitivity and two-interpretation discussion.

findings.description: ABSTRACT TOO LONG AND INCLUDES UNINTERPRETABLE NUMBERS: ~290 words vs 200-word target. Geometric constant detail has no independent value. Per-domain numbers are declared uninterpretable. Uses wrong partition's CMI statistics.
findings.suggestion: Cut to ~220 words. Remove geometric constant sentence. Replace per-domain numbers with 'all five domains.' Use canonical statistics.

findings.description: TITLE PRESUPPOSES UNESTABLISHED CONCLUSION: 'Beyond Competitive Exclusion' implies CE is disproven. Paper shows metric conflates hedging with CE; whether CE exists is unresolved pending activation patching.
findings.suggestion: Alternatives: 'Auditing Feature Absorption: Hedging Dominates the Chanin Metric on JumpReLU SAEs' or 'Decomposing SAE Feature Absorption: Metric Portability, Hedging, and the L0 Phase Transition.'

findings.description: NOTATION INCONSISTENCIES IN COMPANION DOCUMENTS: notation.md has inverted hedging/hierarchy-driven definitions. Glossary cites 'Rajamanickam' (wrong). C4 naming mismatch. Alpha undefined when first used.
findings.suggestion: Fix inversions, author name, C4 naming. Add alpha definition.

metric_gaming_risks: The Chanin metric's shuffled-control failure means ALL absolute absorption rates on JumpReLU SAEs are uninterpretable as measures of competitive exclusion. Mitigations evaluated by this metric on JumpReLU SAEs may be optimizing hedging reduction, not absorption reduction.

The confound decomposition's permissive hedging definition (resolution at ANY higher L0 = hedging) creates a near-tautological result: since L0=176 resolves 99.2% of all false negatives, the maximum possible hierarchy-driven fraction at L0=22 is capped at ~1.5% regardless of underlying mechanism.

The CMI correlation p-value (0.059 uncorrected, 0.236 corrected) combined with post-hoc d'=10 selection from 4 candidates inflates the effective false-positive rate from 5% to ~18.5% under independence.

The phase transition prediction (L0_crit=24.7 vs empirical 22.4) is circular: lambda estimated from empirical data. Rank ordering (the non-circular test) is non-significant (rho=+0.333, p=0.103). Binary classification accuracy (36%) is below the 64% chance baseline.

Using statistics from phase_transition_prediction.json (U=41, p=0.042) rather than cmi_estimation.json (U=28, p=0.045) for the CMI analysis constitutes selecting the more favorable partition's test results -- the absorbed_mean_cmi differs by 5.8%.
novelty_risks: 'Sparse but Wrong' (Chanin and Garriga-Alonso, 2025) already establishes that low L0 causes SAEs to learn wrong features through hedging. The 98.6% figure quantifies this in the absorption metric context -- incremental rather than fundamental.
Cross-domain novelty claim is self-contradictory: 'first measurements' that are simultaneously 'uninterpretable.'
If CMI d'=10 result does not replicate or is explained by probe quality confound, the theoretical pillar reduces to a negative result, removing the paper's highest-novelty claim.

The L0 phase transition is robust but purely empirical. The theoretical explanation (rate-distortion) is inadequately supported. Risk of being perceived as 'we measured L0 vs. absorption and appended unconvincing theory.'
reproducibility_gaps: CMI estimation requires specifying d'=10; a replicator using d'=20 finds the opposite result. No principled justification for d'=10 provided.
Three vocabularies (1,092/1,196/1,204) for the same task; two L0=82 rates (14.39%/15.96%) in the same paper.
9 persistent core words unvalidated by activation patching -- the 'genuine competitive exclusion' claim cannot be verified.
Shuffled-label procedure underspecified: training details, per-domain variance, seed information incomplete.
Threshold sensitivity experiment (141KB) executed but unreported -- selective reporting concern.
No code link for a methods-audit paper where code reproducibility is essential.
